'''Jordan Johnstone''' (born 24 May 1997) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

Johnstone previously played for [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]]<ref name="Jordan Johnstone signs professional deal with Widnes Vikings">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12228/10253482/jordan-johnstone-signs-professional-deal-with-widnes-vikings|title=Jordan Johnstone signs professional deal with Widnes Vikings|publisher=Sky Sports|date=22 April 2016|accessdate=19 October 2016}}</ref> and on loan from [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] at the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Johnstone was born in [[Whitehaven]], [[Cumbria]], [[England]].

==Playing career==
===Whitehaven===
He was in the Cumbria Regional Academy system<ref name=" Cumbrian half-back signs Academy deal">{{cite web|url=http://widnesvikings.co.uk/news/article/47302/cumbrian-half-back-signs-academy-deal|title= Cumbrian half-back signs Academy deal|publisher=Widnes Vikings|date=9 October 2015|accessdate=19 October 2016}}</ref> and played his junior rugby league for the Kells club.<ref name="RL star Jordan's dream becomes a reality">{{cite web|url=http://www.Whitehavenews.co.uk/sport/RL-star-Jordans-dream-becomes-a-reality-584d8e2d-7da3-4da8-b527-ffae457befc8-ds|title=RL star Jordan's dream becomes a reality|publisher=Whitehaven News|date=7 April 2016|accessdate=19 October 2016}}</ref>

He made his début for [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] in 2015.<ref name="Whitehaven to give début to Academy star Jordan Johnstone">{{cite web|url=http://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/Whitehaven-to-give-debut-to-Academy-star-Jordan-Johnstone-3a3d8007-474e-441d-ae0e-e97493ef4830-ds|title=Whitehaven to give début to Academy star Jordan Johnstone|publisher=News & Star|date=15 May 2015|accessdate=19 October 2016}}</ref><ref name="Battered Whitehaven youngster Johnstone desperate to keep his place">{{cite web|url=http://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/whitehaven-rl/article/Battered-Whitehaven-youngster-Johnstone-desperate-to-keep-his-place-545a608c-8fb9-4fec-a82e-004fb5a941d8-ds|title=Battered Whitehaven youngster Johnstone desperate to keep his place|publisher=News & Star|date=3 June 2015|accessdate=19 October 2016}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===
At the end of 2015 he moved to the [[Widnes Vikings]],<ref name=" Cumbrian half-back signs Academy deal"/> making his début in 2016 against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref name="Johnstone signs new two-year deal with Widnes Vikings">{{cite web|url=http://www.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/sport/14445957.Johnstone_signs_new_two_year_deal_with_Widnes_Vikings/|title=Johnstone signs new two-year deal with Widnes Vikings|publisher=Runcorn & Widnes Word|date=22 April 2016|accessdate=19 October 2016}}</ref>

In 2018 Johnstone joined the [[London Broncos]] on loan, and made his [[London Broncos|Broncos]] debut in the victory over the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in Round 2 of the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://londonbroncosrl.com/news/broncos-go-back-to-back-with-dewsbury-win/|title=Broncos go back to back with Dewsbury win|work=London Broncos|date=11 February 2018|accessdate=18 February 2018|archive-date=19 February 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180219090222/https://londonbroncosrl.com/news/broncos-go-back-to-back-with-dewsbury-win/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
Johnstone signed for [[Hull F.C.]] for the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12062703/super-league-jordan-johnstone-aims-to-become-the-master-at-hull-fc|title=Super League: Jordan Johnstone aims to become the master at Hull FC|website=Sky Sports|accessdate=17 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (rejoin)===
In September 2022 it was announced that he had re-joined [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/jordan-johnstone-returns-to-the-vikings/ |title=Jordan Johnstone returns to the Vikings |website=Widnes Vikings |date=22 September 2022 |access-date=31 October 2022}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers (loan)===
On 4 July 2023, it was announced that he had joined [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on loan until the end of the 2023 season as cover for the injured [[Paul McShane (rugby league)|Paul McShane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/66088127.amp|title=Jordan Johnstone: Castleford Tigers sign Widnes Vikings hooker on loan|website=BBC Sport|date=4 Jul 2023 |accessdate=4 Jul 2023}}</ref>
